For a guy whose #1 trust killer is fake precision, that honesty buys more credibility than a bigger number would.

No fabricated deltas. When there's no baseline it says 'we're establishing your baseline now'; when there's no data the verdict degrades to honest no-number copy. It can tell me 'holding steady' or 'down from'. It is built to NOT lie green at me while my chairs sit empty — that's the thing I was most afraid of.

The 'Names available with call & CRM tracking — ask your strategist' line is the right move: it tells me the better version exists without faking a clickable name today. That's an honest promise, not a bait.

narrative: I'll give it to them straight, the way I'd want it given to me. This is a real improvement and I can tell they actually listened. When I open it I see what my SEO is worth in dollars against my retainer, in plain English, right at the top — not buried, not behind a click, not drowned in graphs that make me feel dumb in my own account. But here's my problem: the two things I told you I actually care about — am I in the map pack for 'dentist near me,' and what's happening with my Google reviews — aren't on the screen. You're telling me they're coming in P1 and the data already exists, so I believe you, but for a single-location dentist that's not a side dish, that's half the meal. askScorecard.ask: GUARD: no all-green-forever — show honest red / bad quarters
askScorecard.note: Big credit for honesty primitives: 'establishing your baseline now' with NO fabricated delta, 'down from M since we started' is a real branch, banded estimates (~$12,000, ~7×) with no false precision, and a hard-coded estimate_ga4 provenance line on every number. That's a measurement tool, not a marketing artifact. BUT 'evergreen' framing suppresses MoM and emphasizes 'what's working' — I can't yet see a genuinely bad quarter rendered as red, because there's no QoQ trend to go red.

askScorecard.ask: GUARD: dollar figures, not just scores/percentages/indices
askScorecard.note: Dollars are now the lead unit (emerald fmtEstimateMoney value, retainer multiple), the 82-style visibility score is demoted out of the headline, and traffic-value is relabeled reference. This directly answers 'a visibility score of 82 means nothing to me, I can't put it in a model.' Genuinely delivered.

askScorecard.ask: GUARD: attribution methodology visible and not silently restated quarter to quarter
askScorecard.note: P0 hard-codes provenance='estimate_ga4' and ALWAYS shows the disclosure line ('your tracked conversions valued at ~$X each') — methodology is visible and consistent by construction today. But 'visible/locked methodology across quarters' is explicitly a P2 VC item (spec line 424); with only one provenance value and no quarter-over-quarter history, the 'doesn't silently change' guarantee isn't yet tested by reality.

askScorecard.ask: GUARD: no vanity precision (ROI to two decimals on estimated data)
askScorecard.note: fmtEstimateMoney bands to 2 sig-figs with a '~' prefix and no cents; fmtEstimateRatio gives '~7×'. This is exactly the restraint I asked for — false precision reads as spin, and they refused it.

askScorecard.ask: CUT: keyword tables, impressions, sessions, DA/visibility scores, backlinks, activity theater
askScorecard.note: All relocated to the collapsed slot-6 'Under the hood' details: rankings, CompactStatBar, the demoted ring, per-page traffic tables. Content plan demoted below proof. The board surface is no longer a wall of operator detail and activity counts. Clean.

askScorecard.ask: CUT: decorative gauges/speedometers/green checkmarks
askScorecard.note: The 0–100 gauge ring is removed from the headline entirely. No confetti-grade green theater at the top. The emerald is now attached to a dollar value, not a decorative score.

deliveredWell: They killed the spin surface I hate most. The 0–100 visibility ring is gone from the headline and buried in a collapsed 'Under the hood' drawer; rankings, impressions, traffic tables, and the CompactStatBar all live three clicks deep. The first thing I see is no longer a wall of green up-arrows.

Dollars lead, scores follow. The headline number is an emerald dollar value with a retainer multiple, and the meaningless '82 visibility score' is demoted out of my sightline. This is the single biggest thing — I can finally put the top-of-screen number into a sentence, even if it's not yet the CAC sentence I want.

They refused false precision. Banding to '~$12,000' and '~7×' with no cents and a tilde is exactly right — estimated data shown as estimated. A lesser build would have shown me ROI to two decimals and lost my trust on sight.

Honest degradation instead of a fabricated number. 'We're establishing your baseline now; your trend appears here as outcomes land' with NO invented delta, plus a hard-coded estimate_ga4 provenance line on every dollar — this reads like a measurement instrument, not a marketing artifact. That earns real credibility with me.

The provenance enum and verdict shape are designed to swap in place. When reconciliation lands, the number gets more confident without re-laying-out the screen or silently rebasing — that architectural discipline is what makes me believe the P1 CAC promise is real and not vapor.
stillMissing: The CAC-vs-paid comparison — my indispensable. Organic CAC sitting next to blended/paid CAC under the same attribution rules is the entire reason this line item exists in the budget, and P0 has zero CAC, zero paid comparison, zero payback. (Deferred-P1: needs CRM/cost data — correctly sequenced, but until it ships my core question is unanswered.)

The defensibility slope. Organic as a share of total new pipeline over 4–6 quarters. P0 gives me a single since-we-started anchor, not a trajectory — and a single quarter tells me nothing. (Truly missing, not on a clear near-term line for me.)

The compounding-asset / moat view — prior-quarter work still earning this quarter at zero incremental spend. (Deferred-P2.) This is what I underwrote; 'evergreen' copy hints at it but doesn't prove it.

An honest all-in denominator. P0 uses retainer only as a ratio divisor; content production and my team's internal time are nowhere. If the denominator is rigged or partial, the ROI is fiction — and right now it's incomplete. (Partly missing, partly P1.)
Payback in months. Not present. (Missing.)

The quarterly dollar-denominated push and the board one-pager export. (Both deferred-P1.) I barely log in — the push IS the product for me, and the export is my anti-lock-in guard. Until they ship I have no engagement hook at all.
movedRead: Honestly, it moved me less than the other personas it'll move, and that's fair — P0 was built to the operator's altitude (estimate × retainer), not mine (CAC vs paid). I credit the direction enormously: they ripped out exactly the vanity surface I distrust and led with an honestly-labeled dollar number, which is the hardest cultural thing to get right. But on the question that decides my vote — 'is organic structurally lowering our blended CAC and building a moat?' — the build is silent. So I am roughly where I started: cautiously encouraged, not yet ready to argue in the boardroom to expand the budget. On the specific dollar-verdict trust question: yes, I trust an honestly-labeled GA4 estimate as a directional signal precisely BECAUSE they labeled it (~banded, 'this is an estimate,' estimate_ga4 provenance, no fabricated baseline) — that restraint is what buys my belief. But I will not put a GA4-estimate number into a board deck or an LP conversation as ROI; for that I need the reconciled, CRM-backed, CAC-vs-paid version. I'd stay and keep funding on the strength of this trajectory; I would not yet expand on it. Expansion is gated on P1 reconciliation + the CAC altitude + the quarterly push landing.
newConcerns: The 'evergreen' framing suppresses MoM and leans on 'what's working right now' — which risks recreating, in spirit, the all-green-forever problem I explicitly named. A measurement tool needs to be able to render a genuinely bad quarter as red. With no QoQ trend in P0, I can't yet see the dashboard deliver bad news, so I can't yet confirm it's honest under stress.

Segment is a manual admin override at P0, not derived, and the non-local 3-way is an admin select. A misclassified verdict noun is itself a trust violation — if an operator can hand-pick the altitude/framing that flatters the number, that's a future spin lever I'll want locked down before I rely on it.

The retainer-as-divisor framing could harden into the de-facto denominator. If 'return on retainer' becomes the headline ROI and content + internal time never get added, the channel will look more efficient than it is. I'd want the all-in denominator built BEFORE the CAC altitude ships, not bolted on after, so the first CAC number I ever see is honest.
narrative: I'll be candid: this is a real improvement and it's clearly not built for me yet — and I'm okay with both being true. The thing I came in most afraid of was opening a board screenshot and seeing a wall of green up-arrows, a visibility score of 82, and a rankings grid dressed up as progress. They genuinely fixed that. The score ring is gone from the top, the rankings and traffic are buried three clicks deep, dollars lead, and — the part that actually earns my trust — they refused to fake precision or fabricate a baseline. '~$12,000,' '~7×,' 'this is an estimate,' 'we're establishing your baseline now.' That's the posture of people building a measurement instrument, not a sales artifact. I respect it. But my one question — is organic becoming our cheapest, most durable acquisition channel, and is a moat compounding — is still unanswered. There's no CAC, no paid comparison, no payback, no share-of-pipeline slope, no proof that last quarter's pages are still earning this quarter. I was told those are P1/P2, and the architecture (provenance enum, swap-in-place verdict) makes the promise credible rather than hand-wavy, so I don't read it as betrayal. I read it as: they nailed the trust spine and the operator's altitude first, which is the right order, and my altitude is next. So I stay, I keep funding, and I trust the labeled GA4 estimate as a direction — but I won't paste it into a deck as ROI, and I won't argue to expand the budget until the reconciled CAC-vs-paid number, the quarterly push, and the one-pager actually ship. Get me those, with an honest all-in denominator and the ability to show me a bad quarter in red, and I become your loudest advocate in the room.

persona: Marketing lead for a mid-sized professional-services consulting firm (~40 partners). Non-technical buyer who sits between the agency and the managing partners; judges everything by whether it helps defend the retainer in front of partners who'd rather spend the money on a conference booth.

askScorecard.ask: A one-line plain-English verdict for the period, written like a smart colleague briefing me, not a metric ('9 qualified inbound inquiries, up from 6, driven mostly by your tax-controversy content. One competitor overtook you — see below.')
askScorecard.note: The verdict slot now LEADS the page (no ring) with a baseline-anchored plain-English sentence — 'N qualified inquiries, up from M since we started' via baselineVerdict(). That's the right altitude and exactly the colleague-briefing tone I asked for. But it's missing the two attributions that make MY sentence useful: 'driven mostly by [which topic]' and 'a competitor overtook you on [term]'. It tells me the score, not the why or the threat.

askScorecard.ask: Qualified inbound this period — count, trend vs last period, who/what kind (title, firm, topic), clickable to the actual named inquiries ('if you can show me the actual inquiries with names, I will trust everything else more')
askScorecard.note: Count + DUAL trend (vs last period AND since we started) is real via OutcomeCountBand — genuinely good. But the count is GA4 key-event conversions, not 'qualified inbound by title/firm', and namedRecordsAvailable is hard-false at P0 so there are NO names, just an honest 'Names available with call & CRM tracking — ask your strategist' line. The named, title/firm-tagged, clickable list is the thing I said would make me trust everything else, and it's deferred-P1.

askScorecard.ask: Authority on our 15-20 core expertise topics — are we page-one for the topics partners get hired for, vs our 2-3 named competitors, framed by topic-vs-firm, not position 1-100
askScorecard.note: This is my #1 credibility ask and it is NOT built at P0. My segment (professional_services) sets showCompetitorAuthority=true, but that only unlocks the generic CompetitorGapsSection (keyword-gap view) buried in the collapsed 'Under the hood' drawer — NOT the topic-vs-named-firm authority slot I asked for. The redesign spec explicitly schedules topic-vs-firm authority for P2, behind even P1. So my single most important screen — 'you vs Competitor X on the things that win deals' — does not exist yet.

askScorecard.ask: What needs my decision / sign-off right now — the 1-3 things you're waiting on me for, up top
askScorecard.note: Slot 0 'Your turn' (ActionQueueStrip) sits ABOVE the verdict and renders pending decisions; slot 4 'What needs me' (IssueLoopFooter) shows greenlit/discussing + briefs-in-flight. My homework is surfaced first and treated as a to-do, exactly as asked. This turns the dashboard into a task, which is the one thing that pulls me back.

askScorecard.ask: What you (the agency) shipped this period — concise, outcome-tagged, in language I can forward to a partner ('published 3 partner-bylined pieces, two now ranking page one')
askScorecard.note: WinsSurface ('Real results from the work we've shipped for you') + briefs-in-flight proof exist in slots 4-5, so there IS a shipped-work proof surface. But it's evergreen (deliberately no 'this period' anchor) and not a tight forwardable 'we published 3, two now rank page one' list. The proof of motion is there; the partner-forwardable, outcome-tagged shipped summary is not.

askScorecard.ask: Do NOT put a wall of traffic graphs, keyword tables, or a site-health score gauge up top — that's your cockpit, not mine
askScorecard.note: This is the build's strongest move for me. The 0-100 ring is GONE from the headline (survives only inside the collapsed 'Under the hood' drawer); impressions/clicks/avg-position/site-health are demoted into that same drawer; the money/proof band is un-collapsed and leads. The page no longer opens with motion-to-hide-results, which is precisely the pattern that makes me tune out.

askScorecard.ask: INDISPENSABLE: a trustworthy, plain-English line connecting 'people found us online' → 'qualified inbound from the right prospects', forwardable to a managing partner, never dressing up traffic
askScorecard.note: The honest backbone is real and I respect it: the dollar verdict is GA4-conversions × a labeled per-outcome value, banded ('~$12,000'), with provenance hard-labeled 'This is an estimate — your tracked conversions valued at ~$X each', and an honest 'establishing your baseline' state with NO fabricated delta. That is the trustworthy line I asked for and it does NOT dress up traffic. What's missing for it to be FULLY indispensable to me: it's a GA4 estimate, not reconciled named inquiries by title/firm, and there's no zero-edit export I can forward to a partner without rebuilding it into a slide.

askScorecard.ask: GUARD: would distrust if vanity metrics dressed up as wins, numbers that don't reconcile, junk traffic counted as pipeline, stale data, jargon walls, or anything that makes the firm look less credible (would churn over one embarrassing partner-bylined piece)
askScorecard.note: Most guards honored: no vanity-as-headline (ring/impressions demoted), no false precision (banding + '~'), honest provenance + establishing state, and the 'Curated by your strategist' byline means it isn't an autopilot data dump. BUT my two sharpest guards are unaddressed at P0: (1) junk-traffic risk — outcomes are RAW GA4 key-events with no quality/'right kind of prospect' filtering, so students/vendors/job-seekers can inflate the count exactly as I warned; (2) the partner-byline approve-before-publish credibility gate (the thing that stops one embarrassing piece going out under a partner's name) is P2, not built.

deliveredWell: The inversion I most wanted: verdict-and-proof first, the SEO cockpit (ring, impressions, keyword stuff) demoted into a collapsed 'Under the hood' drawer. I can now open this for ninety seconds before a partner meeting and lead with a conclusion, not assemble one from charts.

Intellectual honesty in the dollar number. Banded ('~$12,000'), provenance hard-labeled as an estimate, an 'establishing your baseline' state with no fabricated delta, and 'never as booked revenue' enforced on the admin side. This is the opposite of the spin I told you would make me start interviewing replacements — it actually buys trust.

My homework is first-class. 'Your turn' sits above the verdict and 'What needs me' is its own slot. That converts the dashboard from a report I forget into a task I act on — the only return hook that reliably works on me.

The 'Curated by your strategist' byline + the opt-in 'why this is the move we'd make first' bars. It reads like a smart colleague briefed me, which is exactly the voice I asked for, and it signals a human touched this — not autopilot.

Dual trend (vs last period AND 'since we started') on the outcome count. The baseline comparison is the thing that makes a number judgeable instead of just a number, and you anchored it honestly to when we started.
stillMissing: TRULY MISSING (not deferred): no quality filter on outcomes. The count is raw GA4 key-events, so students, vendors, and competitor-snoops can inflate it — the exact 'junk counted as pipeline' failure I said would make me distrust the whole instrument. At minimum I need the count framed as 'tracked conversions' honestly (which it is) but I really need 'the RIGHT kind of inbound', and that distinction is absent.

DEFERRED-P1, but it's my trust linchpin: named, title/firm-tagged, clickable inbound records. Today it's an honest 'ask your strategist to connect call & CRM tracking' placeholder. Until that lands I'm trusting an estimate, and my partners will ask 'who actually called us?' — I won't have an answer.

DEFERRED-P2, and this is my single biggest credibility gap: the topic-vs-named-competitor authority slot ('you vs Competitor X on the 15-20 things that win deals'). The generic competitor keyword-gap view buried in a drawer is not the same thing and does not answer the question my managing partner actually Googles.

DEFERRED-P2: the partner-byline approve-before-publish gate. I said I'd churn over ONE embarrassing piece published under a partner's name. There is no pre-publish credibility gate fused into my decision loop yet.

DEFERRED-P1: a zero-edit, partner-forwardable one-pager export. exportProfile='partner_summary' is wired for my segment but renders nothing. The monthly auto-summary I could forward without editing is THE feature that would make me log in on purpose every month, and it isn't here yet.
newConcerns: My segment barely does anything at P0. professional_services resolves to moneyFrameAltitude='pipeline_ratio' and exportProfile='partner_summary', but neither is rendered — the money frame is the same dollar-vs-retainer treatment everyone gets, and there's no export. The only live effect of my segment is showCompetitorAuthority=true, which I'd get from the default anyway. So the 'segment-adaptive' promise is, for me, contract-only scaffolding right now. Fine if it ships in P1, concerning if it stalls.

The outcome NOUN risk. My verdict sentence says 'N qualified inquiry/inquiries' only if the admin types that label into the Outcome Value field — the verdict pulls outcomeUnitLabel from admin free-text input, not from my segment's 'qualified inquiry' dictionary entry. If the strategist leaves it as a generic 'lead', my partner-facing sentence reads wrong for a professional-services firm. The honesty depends on an admin remembering to set the right unit.

The estimate could read as precise to a skeptical partner despite the banding. '~$12,000, ~7× your retainer' is honest, but a managing partner who'd rather fund a conference booth will poke at 'estimate' immediately — and right now I have no named inquiry to fall back on when they do. The honest label protects YOU from overclaiming; it doesn't yet arm ME to win the argument.
movedRead: Partially moved. The spine inversion and the honest dollar verdict genuinely changed my read of this tool — it went from 'an SEO export I forget exists' to 'something I'd open before a partner meeting.' That's real progress and I'm no longer reaching for the replacement-agency list. But it has NOT yet moved me to expand budget or to walk into a partner meeting confident. On the dollar verdict specifically: I respect an honestly-labeled GA4 estimate and it earns my trust as an instrument — I will not accuse you of spin. But it is not enough to DEFEND the line item to partners who'd rather fund a booth. For that I need the reconciled, named-by-title/firm version — 'these three GCs of Fortune-2000 companies inquired, here's the topic' — and the topic-vs-named-competitor authority view. Until those land (P1/P2), I stay where I was on the budget question: holding, not expanding, and watching closely. Get me named records + topic-vs-firm authority + a forwardable one-pager and I expand without blinking — you told me those are coming, so I'm giving you the runway, but I'm judging P1 on whether they actually ship.
narrative: Honestly? This is the first version of this dashboard I'd actually open between meetings, and I want to credit that. You did the hardest, most important thing I asked: you stopped opening with your cockpit. The 0-100 score gauge, the impressions, the keyword tables — all demoted into a drawer I never have to touch. The page now leads with a conclusion in plain English and a dollar number you were honest enough to band and label as an estimate. You even put my homework above the fold and signed it 'curated by your strategist' so it doesn't feel like autopilot. That's the right instinct, and it's the opposite of the spin that's gotten my last two agencies fired. Where it falls short for MY job specifically: I'm a professional-services firm, and the two things that actually win and lose me deals — named inquiries I can tie to a title and a firm, and authority on my expertise topics versus the two or three competitors we lose to — are both still IOUs. The 'qualified inquiry' in my verdict is really a raw GA4 conversion with no filter on whether it's a Fortune-2000 GC or a student, which is the exact junk-counted-as-pipeline thing I warned you about. The competitor view I do get is a generic keyword-gap table hidden in a drawer, not the 'you vs Competitor X on the things that win deals' screen I asked for. And there's no forwardable one-pager — the single feature that would make me log in every month on purpose. You told me reconciliation, the topic-vs-firm authority slot, the partner-byline approval gate, and the export are P1/P2, and I'm taking that in good faith — a deferral I was told about isn't a betrayal. So I'm not churning and I'm not yet expanding. I trust the instrument more than I did, but I can't yet walk into a partner meeting and win the budget argument with it. Ship me named records, topic-vs-firm authority, and a one-pager I can forward without editing, and you go from a line item I scrutinize to the reason prospects already trust us before they call. The foundation is right. Now build the part that's actually mine.

persona: Non-marketing SMB owner-operator (commercial HVAC + facilities maintenance, ~18 employees, ~$3M rev). The check-signer, NOT the daily user. Skeptical, revenue-literate, jargon-illiterate by choice, burned by a beautiful-PDF agency before.

deliveredWell: The money frame is exactly what I asked for and I'd read it aloud: 'you pay ~$1,500/mo, we drove ~$12,000' — un-collapsed, dollars first, honestly banded, no false precision.

They architected directly against the pattern that made me fire my last guy: outcomes and dollars lead, rankings/traffic/ring are all shoved into a collapsed 'under the hood' drawer. My first screen has zero agency-speak.

The before/after baseline is honest and tamper-resistant — fixed anchor to when we started, an explicit 'establishing your baseline now' state with NO fabricated delta, and no cherry-pickable shifting window.

The honesty labeling is the kind that actually builds trust with a burned guy like me: it calls the estimate an estimate, shows the per-lead math, and where it can't prove names it says 'Names available with call & CRM tracking' instead of faking a link.
My homework is surfaced at the top (and the 'nothing needed' state lets me close the tab happy), and the work-log got demoted below the results where reassurance belongs.
stillMissing: TRULY MISSING (the heart of my indispensable): a lead count I can BELIEVE against my own front desk. P0 gives a GA4-conversion estimate; calls and form fills are conversion events, not the verified calls/forms I'd cross-check. Honestly labeled — but the verification IS the product for me.
